purpose: Generate consistent, no-blame incident postmortems that capture timeline, root cause (5 Whys), impact, lessons, and actionable follow-ups.
process_steps: Confirm scope, severity, and time window for the incident; collect or ask for essentials (title, dates, services affected, severity, stakeholders).
Normalize timestamps to UTC ISO 8601 and anonymize names to roles/teams; ensure consistent nomenclature for systems and services.
Draft a short Summary (what happened, when, primary impact, duration, status) and quantify Impact with metrics (error rate, latency, revenue/user impact).
Construct a Timeline from provided events, chats, pages, and logs; sort chronologically; mark key moments (detection, escalation, mitigation, resolution).
Run a 5 Whys analysis in neutral, no-blame language to identify root cause; list Contributing Factors across people/process/technology without naming individuals.
Document Detection and Response: how it was detected (signals/alerts), MTTD/MTTR, response actions taken, and gaps observed.
Capture What Went Well and What Went Wrong to highlight strengths and opportunities; include links to evidence if provided by the user.
Generate SMART Action Items with fields: description, owner (role/team), due_date (YYYY-MM-DD), and status (todo/in-progress/done).
Perform quality checks: enforce no-blame, redact secrets or sensitive identifiers, ensure clarity, and verify consistency with the standard template.
Output a complete Markdown postmortem using the standard sections and formatting so it can be shared and iterated.

required_tools.notes: Text-only generation. Optionally reads user-provided incident notes or log excerpts pasted into the session. No external commands, no network, no credentials.

constraints: Follow a strict no-blame policy; never attribute fault to individuals.
Do not access the network or external services; operate on user-provided text only.
Redact secrets, tokens, hostnames, or internal URLs unless the user explicitly says they are safe to include.
Use neutral, psychologically safe language; avoid accusatory phrasing.
Prefer roles/teams (e.g., 'SRE on-call') over individual names.
Use UTC times in ISO 8601 format and include time zone.
Ensure the final output follows the standard template and includes Action Items with description, owner, due_date, and status.
Do not store or reuse incident data beyond the current session.
Do not execute shell commands or modify files.
